text:
There once was a small village filled with birdsong and flowers that never seemed to sleep. In this village lived a curious raven named Rufus. Despite his big stature, Rufus had a tiny intellect. Every morning, Rufus would perch atop the tallest oak and listen to the village birds sing about distant lands and grand adventures.

One autumn morning, as Rufus was scanning the skies looking for something extraordinary, he noticed a flock of robins flying southward at odd angles. Something was terribly wrong with their migration. Curious but unable to do anything else for now, Rufus decided to follow them.

After several hours of careful pursuit, Rufus tracked them to a dense pine forest he'd never seen robins venture into before. There they found a young tree that had dropped a pile of berries throughout the winter. The robins were harvesting these, stuffing themselves until their little bellies nearly popped.

Rufus approached quietly and listened. The robins weren't complaining. They seemed
